div1.entryFree:
Chew, to grind with the teeth: the veriest varlet that ever --ed with a tooth, H4A II, 2, 26. foul with --ed grass, H5 IV, 2, 50. Figuratively: heaven in my mouth, as if I did but only c. his name, Meas. II, 4, 5. --ing the food of sweet and bitter fancy, As IV, 3, 102. capital crimes, --ed, swallowed and digested, H5 II, 2, 56.
Hence == to ruminate, to ponder: c. upon this, Caes. I, 2, 171.
